2604.12559.title: FABLE: Fine-grained Fact Anchoring for Unstructured Model Editing
2604.12559.paper_summary: Unstructured model editing aims to update models with real-world text, yet existing methods often memorize text holistically without reliable fine-grained fact access. To address this, we propose FABLE, a hierarchical framework that decouples fine-grained fact injection from holistic text generation. FABLE follows a two-stage, fact-first strategy: discrete facts are anchored in shallow layers, followed by minimal updates to deeper layers to produce coherent text. This decoupling resolves the mismatch between holistic recall and fine-grained fact access, reflecting the unidirectional Transformer flow in which surface-form generation amplifies rather than corrects underlying fact representations. We also introduce UnFine, a diagnostic benchmark with fine-grained question-answer pairs and fact-level metrics for systematic evaluation. Experiments show that FABLE substantially improves fine-grained question answering while maintaining state-of-the-art holistic editing performance. Our code is publicly available at https://github.com/caskcsg/FABLE.
2604.12559.paper_summary_zh: 無結構模型編輯旨在用現實世界的文本來更新模型，但現有方法往往整體記憶文本，而無法可靠地獲取細粒度事實。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了FABLE，一個將細粒度事實注入與整體文本生成解耦的層次框架。FABLE遵循兩階段的事實優先策略：離散事實在淺層中固定，然後對深層進行最小更新，以生成連貫的文本。這種解耦解決了整體回憶與細粒度事實獲取之間的不匹配，反映了單向Transformer流，其中表面形式的生成放大而非修正潛在的事實表示。我們還引入了UnFine，一個具有細粒度問答對和事實級別指標的診斷基準，用於系統評估。實驗顯示，FABLE顯著改善了細粒度問答能力，同時保持了最先進的整體編輯性能。我們的代碼已在https://github.com/caskcsg/FABLE上公開。
2604.12559.author: Peng Wang et.al.
2604.12559.authors: Peng Wang, Biyu Zhou, Xuehai Tang, Jizhong Han, Songlin Hu
